fast_path.description: Try the API immediately. The primary first-run evaluation path is a host-authenticated inference request through telnyx.com, so no signup, API key, or email is required. Main REST and MCP surfaces still require a standard Telnyx API key.

primary_path.name: inference
primary_path.note: Recommended first-run evaluation path. Telnyx applies server-side auth and rate limits on behalf of the caller.

demo_endpoints.name: send_sms
demo_endpoints.note: Send a test SMS. No bearer token required. Test numbers only.

demo_endpoints.name: inference
demo_endpoints.note: AI chat completions. No bearer token required.

demo_endpoints.name: tts_demo
demo_endpoints.note: Text-to-speech synthesis. No bearer token required.

demo_endpoints.name: stt_demo
demo_endpoints.note: Speech-to-text transcription. No bearer token required.

demo_endpoints.name: number_lookup
demo_endpoints.note: Carrier lookup. No bearer token required, but it requires hCaptcha so it is not fully autonomous.

demo_endpoints.name: voice_ai_register_web_caller
demo_endpoints.note: Registers a web caller for the Voice AI demo. Requires session_id and email in the body.

fast_path.next_step: Use these demo endpoints first. Only enter the production signup flow if you need real account resources such as API keys, purchased numbers, or authenticated MCP access.

getting_started.summary: Choose one of three first-run paths: zero-signup evaluation, authenticated API or MCP onboarding, or production Voice AI assistant onboarding.

paths.name: Zero-signup first API call
paths.when_to_use: You need to confirm basic Telnyx AI fit before creating an account.
paths.success_signal: A response body plus request-scoped identifiers such as request_id, idempotency_key, or model_id.

paths.name: Authenticated API or MCP onboarding
paths.when_to_use: You need a Telnyx API key for real account resources or authenticated MCP access.
steps: Complete POST /v2/bot_challenge, POST /v2/bot_signup, the email-link exchange, and POST /v2/api_keys
paths.success_signal: A successful bearer-authenticated response from the API or MCP endpoint.

paths.name: Production Voice AI onboarding
paths.when_to_use: You need a Telnyx-hosted AI assistant that can answer a real call.
steps: Create or update the assistant resource
Place one live call through the answer-webhook path and preserve request_id, resource_id, conversation_id, and webhook_delivery_id
paths.success_signal: One live call answered by the configured assistant with reviewable conversation and webhook evidence.

upstream_dependency.required_change: Return a temporary session token directly from POST /v2/bot_signup for bot accounts so they can create an API key without reading email.

notes: Programmatic bot signup uses an obfuscated math challenge from POST /v2/bot_challenge that an LLM can solve without an external solver.
The demo-first fast path is the only no-email autonomous path available today.
Production bot signup still depends on a single-use email magic link unless the upstream account-platform change described in production_signup ships.

risk_classes.read_only: Discovery or inspection only. No account mutation or external side effect is expected.
risk_classes.guarded_write: Billed execution or account-scoped mutation may occur. Confirm intent before mutating state.
risk_classes.live_write: Can send traffic, move money, provision resources, or change regulated production state. Explicit approval is expected before execution.

approval_expectations.none: No extra approval is expected beyond normal caller authorization.
approval_expectations.confirm_before_mutation: Obtain human or policy confirmation before any state-changing or billed execution.
approval_expectations.confirm_before_external_effect: Obtain explicit human or policy approval before sending traffic, spending funds, or provisioning live resources.

approval_paths.none_read_only: Read-only discovery or inspection. Persist returned request identifiers for traceability when needed.
approval_paths.confirm_intent_then_mutate: Capture user or policy confirmation and preserve request-scoped identifiers before any billed or state-changing execution.
approval_paths.explicit_approval_then_execute: Require explicit operator approval, then retain the returned identifiers needed to review external effects, provisioning, or spend.

memory_scopes.stateless: The surface does not retain conversation state unless the caller supplies it.
memory_scopes.host_controlled: Persistence, if any, is owned by Telnyx or the host system rather than the model runtime.
memory_scopes.customer_configured: The customer configures the memory or conversation-retention behavior for this capability.
memory_scopes.app_scoped: State is bounded to the MCP app or workflow contract rather than the full Telnyx account surface.

model_behaviors.host_controlled: No model behavior is exposed at this discovery layer, or execution is not model-driven.
model_behaviors.request_selected: The caller chooses the model at request time.
model_behaviors.customer_configured: The customer selects and configures the model during resource setup.
model_behaviors.app_defined: Model behavior is constrained by the governed app or workflow surface.

audit_identifier_legend.request_id: Request-scoped correlation identifier returned by the surface or retained in logs.
audit_identifier_legend.resource_id: Identifier of the created or mutated Telnyx resource being acted on or reviewed later.
audit_identifier_legend.idempotency_key: Caller-supplied key used to deduplicate retries or confirm operator intent.
audit_identifier_legend.model_id: Model identifier selected or returned for AI inference and evaluation flows.
audit_identifier_legend.conversation_id: Conversation or session identifier for assistant or workflow review.
audit_identifier_legend.webhook_delivery_id: Webhook or event-delivery identifier used to reconcile downstream effects.
